C/C FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY DEFT'S SECURITY DEPOSIT HAD NOT BEEN RETURNED. AFTER HEARING TESTIMONY & ACCEPTING EVIDENCE BY BOTH SIDES THE COURT RULES THAT DEFT WAS NOT ENTITLED TO RETURN OF HER SECURITY DEPOSIT DUE TO THE EXTENT OF THE DAMAGES DONE TO THE PREMISES DURING HER TENANCY THERE.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT MERRITT, LESLIE MOORE, EDWARD MOORE, NATASHA BLAKELY, MONTREAL THE RESTITUTION AND POSSESSION OF THE PREMISES: 48 JENNINGS COURT, STAYED UNTIL SEP-25-2000, LEASE FORFEITED, WRIT MAY ISSUE BUT IS STAYED UNTIL 5PM, 9/25/00. NO STAYS OF EXECUTION. IF DEFT'S COMPLY W/TERMS PLTF WILL SET ASIDE JUDGMENT & DISMISS W/PREJ. IF DEFT'S FAIL TO COMPLY PLTF SHALL UPON WRITTEN DECLARATION ENTER JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFT'S (SEE JUDGMENT FOR SPECIFICS).
